name: SPECTRA: A Traceability Ontology for the 3GPP RAN Standardization Process
description: Reusable OWL 2 ontology for modeling 3GPP RAN standardization document types, relationships, and lifecycle. Released as the companion artifact of a paper submitted to the ISWC 2026 Resources Track (currently under review). The v1.0.0 release contains the ontology TTL, SHACL shapes, PyLODE HTML documentation, the full SpectraCQ v1.0 dataset of 137 competency questions with verbatim company names matching the public 3GPP TDOC_List.xlsx, executable Cypher (one file per CQ) and per-CQ verdict, 15 representative Cypher and 6 representative SPARQL queries, an end-to-end synthetic example, a metadata-only real-world mini instantiation, a metadata-only cross-WG process KG covering RAN1-RAN5, per-WG body-text knowledge graphs (sentence-level rendered text retained with explicit 3GPP source attribution, following the academic redistribution practice established by TSpec-LLM and the GSMA telecom-kg-rel19 release), the sanitized parsing pipeline source, reproducibility tests (verify_release.py; 25/25 deterministic checks across 9 sections), and a validation manifest mapping every quantitative claim in the paper to its supporting JSON evidence file. Raw Neo4j instance dumps and VectorDB embeddings are not bundled because they exceed the Zenodo size cap and are regenerable from the released body-text KGs and pipeline source.

isPartOf.name: SPECTRA: A Traceability Ontology for the 3GPP RAN Standardization Process

publisher.name: International Semantic Web Conference (ISWC), Resources Track
